methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $9.7B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

data.narrative:
Neuralink, a Series C-stage AI & Software company, is valued at $9.7B post-money following $1.3B+ in total fundraising. The peer universe consists of 9 private companies and 1 public comparable (Palantir), all operating in the AI & Software sector at similar or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $9.7B last post-money valuation, implying $16.5B. Peer-Median analysis examines 6 same-sector, same-stage comparables—Figure AI ($39.0B), Skild AI ($14.0B), Luma AI ($4.0B), ElevenLabs ($3.0B), Runway ($1.5B), and one additional peer—yielding a median of $3.5B with a range spanning $1.5B to $39.0B. The wide peer dispersion reflects heterogeneity in market perception and commercialization progress within frontier AI.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $3.0B to $19.0B, with a median of $10.0B. The current $9.7B valuation aligns closely with the triangulated median. Key uncertainties include the relevance of the peer set given Neuralink's distinct neurotechnology application focus versus pure software comparables, and the applicability of stage-progression multiples in a frontier-stage market where typical progression patterns may not hold. The wide range reflects genuine uncertainty about long-term commercialization timelines and competitive positioning within this nascent segment.
